summary: End-to-end testing of WatchHub Smartwatch Leaderboard (Angular 20 + FastAPI mock). Backend pytest suite (17 tests) all pass. Frontend Playwright run validated health page, login (valid+invalid), register, route guard, telemetry validation+submit, challenge join, leaderboard with podium/medals/masking, admin task creation, trigger-ranking, and logout. Auth interceptor confirmed attaching both X-Session-Id and Authorization headers on protected calls. One minor UX bug in the error interceptor and one minor missing data-testid noted.

ui_bugs.issue: Invalid login credentials show snackbar 'Session expired - please log in again' instead of an 'Invalid credentials' / INVALID_CREDENTIALS-specific message. The 401 returned by POST /api/auth/login is treated like a session-expiry 401 and triggers the generic session-expired toast (the snack appears twice on a single failed attempt).

data-testid="podium-card" is not rendered on /challenges/:id participant podium (count=0 returned). It IS rendered on the /leaderboard page. If podium is intended on challenge-detail, add the data-testid; otherwise update the test plan note.

action_items: Update error.interceptor.ts: when status===401 AND request URL ends with /api/auth/login (or response detail.code === 'INVALID_CREDENTIALS'), show a credential-specific message ('Invalid email or password') instead of the generic 'Session expired - please log in again'. Also ensure the snackbar is only opened once per failure (currently shows twice).

(Optional) Add data-testid="podium-card" to the top-3 participant cards on /challenges/:id if a podium is supposed to be shown there, OR clarify in the test spec that podium-card only exists on /leaderboard.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend /api/user/{user_id} POST telemetry: pydantic gt=0 returns 422 (Unprocessable Entity), but the requirement says 'inline validation' — frontend correctly enforces a client-side mat-error 'Steps must be greater than 0' before the API call. Good.

Backend register endpoint persists to db.json on every write — fine for mock, but concurrent writes only guarded by a thread lock; under multi-worker uvicorn deployment this would race. Acceptable for current single-worker dev mode.
Auth interceptor sets BOTH X-Session-Id and Authorization: Bearer. Server accepts either; not a bug but slightly redundant.
ErrorInterceptor double-snackbar on a single failed login (observed two identical toasts). Likely missing return/throwError dedupe or subscribing twice.
db.json is updated by tests (new users, tasks, etc.) and not cleaned up — over many iterations the seed grows. Consider an /api/_reset endpoint or test fixtures that delete TEST_ prefixed rows.

success_rate.frontend: ≈95% (all 13 listed scenarios pass; 1 UX wording bug, 1 missing optional data-testid)

seed_data_creation: Tests created several TEST_ tasks, devices, and play_<hex>@example.com registered users via /api/auth/register. They are persisted to /app/backend/db.json. No cleanup endpoint exists; recommend not running suite repeatedly without reset.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py - run with: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<public-url> pytest backend/tests/backend_test.py --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml. Frontend uses Angular Material; data-testids are present on all critical elements. NOTE: register-role is a mat-radio-group (not a select); default value is 'User' (ROLE_USER) - to pick admin click the 'Admin' radio. The user-menu logout option does not have a data-testid; click element with text 'Sign out' after clicking [data-testid='nav-user-menu']. Leaderboard podium-card data-testid exists on /leaderboard only.
rca of the issue: Login-failure snackbar text: The ErrorInterceptor likely contains a branch like `if (err.status === 401) snack.open('Session expired - please log in again')` without checking the request URL or err.error.detail.code. Both the auth/login 401 (INVALID_CREDENTIALS) and a protected-route 401 (UNAUTHENTICATED) thus map to the same toast. Fix: special-case `/auth/login` 401 to read err.error.detail.message (server already returns 'Invalid email or password' with code INVALID_CREDENTIALS) and skip the auto-logout/redirect for that case. Also ensure the interceptor pipes one error once - double-toast suggests the error stream is being subscribed twice (possibly because both ErrorInterceptor and login component show the snack on subscribe error).
